title: Sagina alexandrae
intro: Sagina alexandrae, also known as Alexandra's pearlwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to New Zealand that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. The health benefits of Sagina alexandrae include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and reduce cell damage caused by oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alexandra's pearlwort is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and it is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Sagina alexandrae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Sagina, which includes a group of small, herbaceous plants commonly known as pearlwort. The species is native to New Zealand and is found in alpine and montane regions. The exact classification of Sagina alexandrae is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Caryophyllales, Family: Caryophyllaceae, Genus: Sagina, Species: S. alexandrae.
